_meta.note: Daemon-backed one-call rule-pack import (TCE-ERG-PACK). Imports an embedded pack by name; with activate=true + scope, promotes its rules to Active and activates them so the agent gets expert signal extraction without authoring rule JSON. TB-5: the scope requirement is CONDITIONAL (required only when activate=true), so scope stays optional in the MCP input schema required[]; the conditional is documented in the tool/param descriptions and enforced by the daemon scope-required teaching error (ScopeInvalid) rather than a JSON-Schema if/then. TB-9: the `pack` param carries a JSON-Schema enum of all EIGHT seed packs (generic.terminal, apt, cargo, npm, pytest, gcc, make, cleanup) so the schema can never advertise fewer packs than the daemon accepts. An unknown pack name returns an invalid_params error listing the known packs.

invariants: pack is required and must be a known pack name
the pack JSON-Schema enum lists all eight seed packs including cleanup (TB-9)
activate=true requires scope; missing scope is rejected with scope_invalid
an unknown pack name returns invalid_params listing the known packs
activated is a subset of imported and empty when activate=false
no raw stream content is returned; imported/activated are rule ids
